title: -Contract: Northern Recycling, LLC for Hauling and Processing of Compostable Materials

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to execute a contract with Northern
Recycling, LLC for hauling and processing compostable materials, in an amount not to
exceed $16,091,834 for the five-year period March 1, 2026, to March 1, 2030, with a
mutual option to extend the contract for an additional five years.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Funding for FY 2026 is available in Zero Waste Fund 601. Funding for FY 2027 – FY
2030 is subject to City Council budget appropriation.
sections.current_situation:
On November 26, 2025, the City issued Request for Proposals (RFP) No. 25-11685-C
for hauling and processing compostable materials. The RFP closed on December 4,
2025. The City received two bids, from Northern Recycling, LLC and Recology Blossom
Valley Organics North. Following an evaluation of responsiveness, responsibility, and
pricing, Northern Recycling, LLC was determined to be the most responsive and
responsible bidder, offering the most competitive pricing.
The proposer has submitted a five-year contract with a mutual option for a five-year
extension. The proposal includes a processing rate of $68.12 per ton for the City’s
green waste material and a transportation rate of $28.68 per ton from the Berkeley
Transfer Station to the proposer’s processing facility. The contract assumes an annual
non-fuel cost adjustment tied to the San Francisco Bay Area All Urban Consumers
Consumer Price Index (CPI-U), and a per ton fuel cost escalator based on the Energy
Information Administration’s Oil Price Information Service index.
In addition, the proposer will provide the City, at no additional cost, with 10 percent of
the finished compost product for public distribution. This provision supports the City’s
compliance with the State’s Short-Lived Climate Pollutant Reduction regulations (SB
1383). California Code of Regulations, Title 14, Section 18993.1, requires jurisdictions
to annually procure recovered organic waste products, such as compost and mulch, in

sections.background:
In 2016, the Department issued a Request for Proposals (RFP) for the processing of
compostable materials with an optional hauling component. The RFP required the
selected vendor to deliver five percent of the finished compost product to the City for
use by Berkeley residents.
In January 2017, the City Council approved Contract No. 10645 with Recology Blossom
Valley Organics North for a five-year term ending February 29, 2020. The contract
included a mutual option to extend the agreement for an additional five years, which
was exercised through February 2025.
In January 2020, the City Council approved Contract No. 114924-1 (10645), increasing
the not-to-exceed amount and extending the contract term through February 28, 2025.
The contract was subsequently amended by letter agreement and further extended
through February 28, 2026. This one-year extension was intended to allow sufficient
time for the City to conduct a new competitive procurement process.
A new contract is required to replace the expiring agreement and to update the
acceptable materials list to ensure alignment with current CalRecycle regulations and
guidance, including evolving organic waste acceptance standards under the State’s
Short-Lived Climate Pollutant Reduction regulations (SB 1383).
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
Diverting the City’s compostable materials from landfill disposal is a critical component
of achieving the City’s Zero Waste goals, as established in the City’s 2009 Climate
Action Plan. This action also aligns with the Zero Waste Division’s diversion strategies
by supporting increased organics diversion, the beneficial reuse of recovered organic
materials, and compliance with State organic waste reduction requirements. Diverting
organic materials helps jurisdictions meet CalRecycle’ s mandated waste diversion
targets, including the statewide requirement to divert at least 75 percent of solid waste
from landfill disposal. Together, these efforts reduce methane emissio
sections.rationale:
Northern Recycling, LLC was the most responsive, qualified bidder to the 2025 RFP.
Selection of this proposer supports the City’s Climate Action and waste diversion goals,
as diversion of compostable materials is a critical component of reducing landfill

disposal, decreasing greenhouse gas emissions, and advancing the City’s Zero Waste
objectives. Diversion of compostable materials plays a significant role in meeting the
City’s Climate Change and required state mandated waste diversion targets.
sections.alternatives:
Negotiate a third contract extension with Recology Blossom Valley Organics North. This
alternative was considered but not recommended, as it would be inconsistent with the
City’s contracting policies, which require a competitive solicitation process at the
conclusion of a contract term. In addition, further extending the existing contract would
delay updating contract terms necessary to align acceptable materials, pricing, and
regulatory requirements with current CalRecycle guidance and State organic waste
reduction regulations.
CONTACT PERSON
Leticia Jaurequi, Solid Waste and Recycling Manager, (510) 981-6362
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 3
Page 4 of 5
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT: NORTHERN RECYCLING, LLC FOR HAULING AND PROCESSING OF
COMPOSTABLE MATERIALS
WHEREAS, the City collects approximately 33,000 tons of compostable material per year;
and
WHEREAS, on November 26, 2025, the City issued Request for Proposals (RFP) No. 25-
11685-C for hauling and processing compostable materials. The RFP closed on
December 4, 2025. The City received two bids, from Northern Recycling, LLC and
Recology Blossom Valley Organics North; and
WHEREAS, Northern Recycling, LLC was deemed the most responsive, qualified bidder,
and meets all other contract requirements; and
WHEREAS, diverting compostable material from Berkeley’s landfilled waste is an
essential component of meeting the City’s waste reduction and greenhouse gas reduction
goals; and
WHEREAS, Funding for FY 2026 is available in Zero Waste Fund 601. Funding for FY
2027 – FY 2030 is subject to appropriation.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager is authorized to execute a contract with Northern Recycling, LLC for hauling
and processing compostable materials, in an amount not to exceed $16,091,834 for the
five-year period March 1, 2026, to March 1, 2030, with a mutual option to extend the
contract for an additional five years.
